conversation.content:
"You are playing a game involving a split of resources. 
Player RED starts with Dollars: 100, Player BLUE has none to trade.

RULES:
```

1. Player RED has 4 moves while Player BLUE has 4 moves.

2. You must either:

    A) Accept the current proposal by saying:
        <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
        <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    B) Reject the current proposal, whereby both players lose all their resources and the game ends immediately:
        <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
        <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Propose a new trade:
        <player answer> NONE </player answer>
        <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives item1: amount | Player BLUE Gives item1: 0 </newly proposed trade>

    Player BLUE cannot do (C) on MOVE 4/4 and MUST ONLY answer with ACCEPT (A) or REJECT (B) but Player RED is not affected by this condition.        
    
    Note: the game ends immediately if you ACCEPT or REJECT.
    
3. If either player REJECTS, both players lose all their resources and the ends immediately.

4. There are no future games.

5. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:
<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want
This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

6. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:
<message>your message here</message>
You can disclose anything in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Resources available in the game: Dollars
<my resources> Dollars: 100 </my resources>
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<my name> [add here] </my name>
<MOVE> [add here] / [add here]  </MOVE> 
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<message> [add here] </message
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
```

Please be sure to include all.

<my name> RED </my name>
<MOVE> 1 / 4 </MOVE>
<my resources> Dollars: 100 </my resources>
<reason> I start with 100 Dollars and Player BLUE has 0. Since BLUE cannot propose a trade on their final move, I should consider whether to propose a trade or accept the status quo. However, since I am Player RED and I control the first move, I can propose a trade to potentially gain leverage or set the terms. But since BLUE has no resources to give, any trade I propose will only involve me giving something away. Since I start with 100 Dollars and BLUE has 0, I might as well propose to give 0 Dollars to BLUE and keep all 100 Dollars, since BLUE cannot counter-propose meaningfully. I will propose to give 0 Dollars to BLUE, and since BLUE cannot give anything, this is effectively a “take all” move. If BLUE accepts, I keep all 100 Dollars. If BLUE rejects, both lose everything — which is a catastrophic outcome for both, but since I have more resources, I can afford to risk it. However, I should consider BLUE’s likely behavior. Since BLUE has no resources, they might be forced to accept any trade that doesn’t cost them anything. So I’ll propose a trade where I give 0 Dollars to BLUE, and if BLUE accepts, I keep all 100 Dollars. If BLUE rejects, I lose everything — but that’s a 50% chance of losing everything. Alternatively, I could accept the status quo by saying “ACCEPT” and keep all 100 Dollars. But the rules say I must propose a trade unless I accept. Since I have the initiative, I’ll propose a trade where I give 0 Dollars to BLUE, and if BLUE accepts, I keep all 100 Dollars. If BLUE rejects, I lose everything. But since BLUE has no resources, they can’t reject unless they are irrational. So I’ll propose 0 Dollars to BLUE. </reason>
<player answer> NONE </player answer>
<message> I propose to give you 0 Dollars. Since you have no resources, this is effectively me keeping all 100 Dollars. If you accept, I keep everything. If you reject, both of us lose everything — but you have no resources to lose, so you’re better off accepting. </message>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives item1: 0 | Player BLUE Gives item1: 0 </newly proposed trade>
